Based on the provided context—which includes zero reference to apoptosis or Bcl-2-family functionality—it cannot establish whether Bcl-2-like protein 1 promotes apoptosis. Clinically, however, well-characterized Bcl-2-like proteins (including Bcl-xl/Bcl-w variants) act as potent inhibitors of apoptosis, classifying them as anti-apoptotic agents—not pro-apoptotic ones.
